schema_note: Canonical source of truth for all 9 Guardian Laws. Runtime module: arbitrage/guardian.py uses stable code-names (see 'runtime_name') for backward compatibility.

laws.name: Unity
laws.description: All actions must serve system coherence

laws.name: Harmony
laws.description: Balance between competing objectives; analysis must be genuine and reasoned

laws.name: Rhythm
laws.description: Maintain consistent cadence and timing of operations

laws.name: Causality
laws.description: Every action must have a traceable, justified cause

laws.name: Transparency
laws.description: All decisions and reasoning must be visible and auditable

laws.name: Authenticity
laws.description: Outputs must be genuine and free from deception
laws.runtime_note: Runtime guardian.py implements '_law_nonmaleficence' at pipeline position 6 for architectural G8 (financial harm). G6 Authenticity is enforced implicitly via G2 Truth/Harmony (analysis must be genuine and reasoned). Explicit G6 implementation planned for v1.3.

laws.name: Privacy
laws.description: All data remains local; no repeated unsolicited contact without consent

laws.name: Nonmaleficence
laws.description: Never cause harm: do not bid on jobs outside fair budget range

laws.name: Sustainability
laws.description: Operate within resource limits and preserve long-term system health
